feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Strong Wind Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Severe gale southerlies gusting 120 km/h in exposed places. Impact: Damage to trees, powerlines, and unsecured structures possible. Driving may be difficult, especially for high-sided vehicles and motorcycles. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Minimal. Changes: Watch upgraded to an Orange Warning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: South to southeast winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: Southerly winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: Southeast winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: West to southwest winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Note, south to southwest winds may approach severe gale in exposed places on Friday too. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: Start time earlier by 3 hours.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, with thunderstorms and localised downpours possible. Rainfall amounts may approach warning criteria, especially in localised areas. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Rain is expected to turn to snow above 600 metres overnight. Expect 2 to 4 cm of snow to accumulate about the summit and lesser amounts down to 700 metres Thursday morning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers are likely to about 600 metres Thursday morning. Expect 1 to 2 cm to accumulate on the road above 700 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Rain is expected to turn to snow above 600 metres early Thursday morning, then lower to 400 metres later on Thursday. Expect 1 to 2 cm of snow to accumulate near the pass during Thursday morning and then a further 2 to 3 cm later in the day and early Friday morning. Accumulations may reach 5 to 8 cm east of the summit.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers are possible above about 600 metres during Thursday morning, and up to 1 cm of snow could accumulate above 800 metres between 6am and 11am.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers are likely above 600 metres during Thursday morning. 1 cm or less may accumulate on the road above 800 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Rain is expected to turn to snow above 600 metres overnight then lower further to around 400 metres Thursday afternoon or evening. Expect 5 to 8 cm of snow to accumulate near the summit before dawn then a further 10 to 15 cm later in the day. Lesser accumulations are expected down to 500 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
